attractions.name: Kenai Fjords National Park Day Cruise
attractions.season: Operators run mid-March to late Sept; full fjord runs Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Boat trip from Seward to tidewater glaciers (Aialik, Holgate) past orcas, humpbacks, puffins and sea lions.
attractions.tip: Take the 7.5-8hr Aialik/Northwestern boat, not the 4hr bay tour - the 4hr never reaches a tidewater glacier.

attractions.name: Harding Icefield Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly late June-early Oct; snow patches into July
attractions.blurb: 8.2mi round trip, ~3,000ft gain from Exit Glacier to a rim view over the 700 sq mi Harding Icefield.
attractions.tip: Start by 8am; the last mile holds snow into July and clouds usually swallow the rim by afternoon.

attractions.name: Alaska Railroad Coastal Classic (Seward Depot)
attractions.season: Daily mid-May to mid-Sept only; no winter Seward service
attractions.blurb: 4.5hr rail run Anchorage-Seward through Turnagain Arm, Placer Valley and the Kenai Mountains; no road parallels much of it.
attractions.tip: Sit on the left leaving Anchorage. It arrives 11:05am - too late for a morning fjords cruise, so overnight in Seward.

attractions.name: Exit Glacier
attractions.season: Road open ~late May-mid Nov; winter ski/fatbike/snowmachine access
attractions.blurb: Only road-accessible piece of Kenai Fjords NP; paved 1mi loop to the outwash plain, 2.2mi loop to the Glacier Overlook.
attractions.tip: Year markers along the road show the retreat - a free, brutal climate visual. Parking fills 11am-3pm.

attractions.name: Homer Spit
attractions.season: Shops/charters May-Sept; mostly shuttered Oct-Apr
attractions.cost: Free to walk; $ for everything on it
attractions.blurb: 4.5mi gravel bar jutting into Kachemak Bay: boat harbor, charter row, boardwalk shacks, campgrounds, ferry dock.
attractions.tip: Everything - halibut charters, water taxis, Halibut Cove ferry, AMHS - leaves from the Spit, not downtown Homer.
attractions.duration_note: half-day, or your whole Homer base

attractions.name: Prince William Sound 26 Glacier Cruise
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-Sept
attractions.blurb: Fast catamaran from Whittier into Blackstone Bay and Barry Arm past a wall of tidewater and hanging glaciers.
attractions.tip: Build the Whittier tunnel schedule into your day - miss your slot and you miss the boat by a full hour.

attractions.name: Homer Bear-Viewing Flights (Katmai / Lake Clark)
attractions.season: Late May-mid Sept; sedge-flat bears Jun, salmon bears Jul-Aug
attractions.blurb: Float/wheel plane day trips across Cook Inlet to Chinitna Bay, Silver Salmon Creek or Hallo Bay to walk near brown bears.
attractions.tip: Cheaper and far more reliable than Brooks Falls. Weather cancels often - budget two possible days, book the first.

attractions.name: Alaska SeaLife Center
attractions.season: Open year-round, daily in summer
attractions.blurb: Alaska's only public aquarium and marine rehab hospital: Steller sea lions, puffin dive tank, octopus, wild-animal ICU.
attractions.tip: The rainy-day fallback when boats cancel. Behind-the-scenes encounters (puffin/octopus) sell out weeks ahead.

attractions.name: Homer Halibut Charters
attractions.aka: Halibut Fishing Capital of the World
attractions.season: Charter season roughly mid-May to mid-Sept
attractions.blurb: Full-day halibut (and rockfish/king salmon combo) charters out of Homer Harbor into lower Cook Inlet.
attractions.tip: Charter halibut limits are size-restricted - expect one small fish plus one any-size. Get fish processed and shipped same day.

attractions.name: Kachemak Bay State Park
attractions.season: Trails snow-free ~June-Sept; water taxis run May-Sept
attractions.blurb: 400,000-acre roadless state park across Kachemak Bay - glaciers, 80mi of trail, public-use cabins, no road in.
attractions.tip: Book the water taxi first; the boat sets your pickup time and that dictates which trail you can actually finish.

attractions.name: Seward
attractions.season: Year-round; tourism May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Port town of ~2,700 at the head of Resurrection Bay; base for Kenai Fjords, Exit Glacier and salmon charters.
attractions.tip: Mile 0 of the Iditarod Trail. Lodging books out 6+ months for July 4 (Mount Marathon) - avoid or plan around it.

attractions.name: Holgate Glacier
attractions.season: Reachable by tour boat May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Actively calving tidewater glacier in a steep-walled arm of Kenai Fjords; the reliable calving stop on longer cruises.
attractions.tip: Boats idle 20-30 min here. Watch the water below the face - most calving is announced by a crack you hear first.
attractions.duration_note: a stop on the day cruise

attractions.name: Homer
attractions.season: Year-round; visitor season May-Sept
attractions.blurb: End of the Sterling Highway (Mile 179): arts-and-fishing town of ~5,500 on a bluff above Kachemak Bay.
attractions.tip: Stay on the bluff downtown for food and galleries, not the Spit, unless you want harbor noise at 5am.

attractions.name: Kenai River Float, Cooper Landing
attractions.season: Guided floats roughly mid-May to late Sept
attractions.blurb: Half-day raft down the glacier-blue upper Kenai through Kenai Canyon; bald eagles, dip-netting bears, spawning sockeye.
attractions.tip: Ask for the Kenai Canyon section (Class II-III) over the flat scenic float; dry bibs are supplied, wear wool under them.

attractions.name: Grewingk Glacier Lake Trail
attractions.season: Water-taxi accessible May-Sept; snow-free June-Oct
attractions.blurb: Flat 3.2mi walk from Glacier Spit to an iceberg-strewn lake below Grewingk Glacier in Kachemak Bay SP.
attractions.tip: Ask the water taxi for Glacier Spit drop / Saddle Trail pickup so you walk it one way and add the hand tram.

attractions.name: Kenai River at Soldotna
attractions.season: First sockeye run late Jun; big second run mid-Jul; silvers Aug-Sept
attractions.blurb: The salmon river most Alaskans mean: bank boardwalks in Soldotna city parks plus a huge drift-boat guide fleet.
attractions.tip: Kenai king salmon runs are collapsed - kings are usually closed or catch-and-release. Check ADF&G emergency orders first.

attractions.name: Resurrection Bay Sea Kayaking
attractions.season: Guided paddles mid-May to mid-Sept
attractions.blurb: Guided sea kayaking from Lowell Point beach; sea otters, harbor seals, and rainforest shoreline with no motor noise.
attractions.tip: The premium version is a water-taxi drop into Aialik Bay to paddle icebergs at a glacier face - book weeks out.

attractions.name: Kenai-Russian River Confluence
attractions.season: Two sockeye runs: mid-June and mid-July to early Aug
attractions.blurb: Alaska's densest bank fishery - anglers shoulder-to-shoulder flipping for sockeye where the Russian meets the Kenai.
attractions.tip: Brown bears work this bank; clean fish only at the designated stations and carry your catch in a sealed bag.

attractions.name: Mount Marathon Trail
attractions.season: Hikeable roughly June-Oct; race is every July 4
attractions.blurb: Brutally steep climb to a 2,974ft race point straight above Seward; venue of the July 4 mountain race run since 1915.
attractions.tip: Use the Jeep/hiker trail, not the racers' cliff route. Race day fills every bed in town - book a year out or skip.

attractions.name: Portage Glacier & Begich Boggs Visitor Center
attractions.season: Visitor center summer only (hours vary by year); boat tours Jun-Sept
attractions.blurb: Forest Service center on Portage Lake; the glacier has retreated out of sight, so you need the boat tour to see it.
attractions.tip: It's on the Whittier tunnel road - pair with a tunnel slot. Byron Glacier Trail nearby is a flat 1.4mi to ice caves.

attractions.name: College Fjord
attractions.season: Cruise ships and long day boats Jun-Sept
attractions.blurb: Prince William Sound arm walled with glaciers named for Ivy League colleges; Harvard Glacier fills the head.
attractions.tip: Most people see this from a repositioning cruise's last sea day into Whittier - be on deck by 5pm, not at dinner.

attractions.name: Chiswell Islands
attractions.season: Seabirds nesting May-Aug; sea lions year-round
attractions.blurb: Rock stacks off Kenai Fjords holding Steller sea lion haulouts plus tufted/horned puffins and common murres.
attractions.tip: Only the longer (7.5hr+) Seward cruises go this far out. Puffins leave the colonies by late August.
attractions.duration_note: a stop on the day cruise

attractions.name: Russian River Falls
attractions.season: Salmon leaping the falls mid-June and mid-July to Aug
attractions.blurb: Easy 4.6mi round-trip trail to viewing decks where thousands of sockeye jump a cascade; bears fish it too.
attractions.tip: Trailhead is inside Russian River Campground at Sterling Hwy MP 52. Go early - bears are most active before 9am.

attractions.name: Halibut Cove
attractions.season: Galleries and ferry Jun-early Sept only
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk artists' colony of ~35 people across Kachemak Bay; no roads, galleries built on pilings over the water.
attractions.tip: The classic is the Danny J from Homer Spit: afternoon sailing with dinner at The Saltry, back after 10pm. Reserve.

attractions.name: Anton Anderson Memorial Tunnel
attractions.season: Open year-round; shorter hours Oct-Apr, closed overnight
attractions.cost: $13 round trip, paid heading into Whittier
attractions.blurb: 2.5mi single-lane tunnel shared by cars and trains under Maynard Mountain - the only road into Whittier.
attractions.tip: One direction at a time: out of Whittier on the hour, in on the half hour. Check the DOT schedule before you drive.

attractions.name: Kenai Fjords Glacier Lodge
attractions.season: Open roughly early June-early Sept
attractions.blurb: 16-cabin lodge inside Kenai Fjords NP on Pedersen Lagoon; boat-in only, guided kayaking and glacier hikes included.
attractions.tip: The only lodging inside the park. One night is really a half-day - go two nights or the boat transit eats the trip.

attractions.name: Cooper Landing
attractions.season: Year-round; guides and lodges May-Sept
attractions.blurb: String of lodges and raft guides at the Kenai Lake outlet, at the Sterling/Seward highway split - the peninsula's crossroads.
attractions.tip: Expect delays MP 45-60: the Cooper Landing bypass (Juneau Creek) is a multi-year build with no near-term finish.
attractions.duration_note: a stop or a base night

attractions.name: Seldovia
attractions.season: Ferry/water taxi May-Sept; year-round air
attractions.blurb: Roadless village of ~220 across Kachemak Bay with Russian and Alutiiq roots and a surviving historic boardwalk.
attractions.tip: Do it as a day trip: fast water taxi over, walk the boardwalk and Otterbahn Trail, fly or ferry back the same day.

attractions.name: Portage Pass Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly late June-Oct
attractions.blurb: Steep 2mi climb from the Whittier side of the tunnel to a pass overlooking Portage Glacier and Portage Lake.
attractions.tip: Best free view in Whittier and it fills your tunnel wait perfectly - the trailhead is minutes from the portal.

attractions.name: Alaska Islands & Ocean Visitor Center
attractions.season: Daily in summer; reduced Tue-Sat in winter
attractions.blurb: Free federal center on the 2,500-island Alaska Maritime Refuge, with a full-scale research-ship bunkroom and seabird exhibits.
attractions.tip: Free, excellent, and the trailhead for Beluga Slough. Best rainy-day stop in Homer; ask about ranger beach walks.

attractions.name: Lost Lake Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free ~July-Sept; classic winter fatbike/ski route
attractions.blurb: 15mi point-to-point over alpine tundra to a high blue lake above Seward; 7mi each way from the south trailhead.
attractions.tip: Two cars lets you go Primrose to Lost Lake downhill. Snow lingers at the lake well into July most years.

attractions.name: Skilak Lake Road
attractions.season: Gravel road generally passable May-Oct
attractions.blurb: 19mi gravel loop off the Sterling Highway through Kenai NWR: turquoise Skilak Lake, moose, campgrounds, short trails.
attractions.tip: Skilak Lookout Trail (2.5mi rt) is the payoff. Check the Kenai NWR Visitor Center in Soldotna for road and bear reports.

attractions.name: Kenai Old Town & Holy Assumption Russian Orthodox Church
attractions.season: Bluff open year-round; church tours summer only
attractions.blurb: 1895 Russian Orthodox church, a National Historic Landmark, on the Dena'ina bluff above the Kenai River mouth.
attractions.tip: Walk to Erik Hansen Scout Park behind it - beluga whales work the river mouth on incoming tides in July-August.

attractions.name: Whittier
attractions.season: Year-round, but the town only functions May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Cruise port and Prince William Sound gateway where nearly all ~270 residents live in one 14-story building, Begich Towers.
attractions.tip: Notoriously rainy and windy - it is a launch point, not a stay. The derelict Buckner Building is the town's oddity.

attractions.name: Seward Harbor & Cruise Terminal
attractions.aka: Dale R. and Carol Ann Lindsey Alaska Railroad Terminal
attractions.season: Cruise calls mid-May to mid-Sept; harbor year-round
attractions.blurb: Seward's small-boat harbor and cruise dock, beside the Alaska Railroad depot; every fjords cruise and charter departs here.
attractions.tip: The new terminal sits beside the Alaska Railroad depot - cruise-to-train transfers are a walk, not a shuttle.

attractions.name: Ninilchik Village & Transfiguration of Our Lord Chapel
attractions.season: Grounds open year-round; halibut charters May-Sept
attractions.blurb: 1901 white-and-blue Orthodox church on a bluff over Cook Inlet above a Russian-Alutiiq village of weathered cabins.
attractions.tip: Clear days frame Mt. Redoubt and Iliamna across the inlet. Nearby Deep Creek launches halibut boats by tractor off the beach.

attractions.name: Pratt Museum
attractions.season: Daily 10-6 May-early Sept; Tue-Sat 11-4 winter
attractions.blurb: Homer's natural-history and culture museum: Kachemak Bay ecology, Alutiiq material, and a hard-eyed Exxon Valdez exhibit.
attractions.tip: Small but genuinely good; the botanical garden and forest trail behind it are free even when the museum is shut.

attractions.name: Hope
attractions.season: Road open year-round; businesses May-Sept
attractions.blurb: 1896 gold-rush village of ~80 at the end of a 17mi spur off the Seward Highway on Turnagain Arm; log storefronts, no crowds.
attractions.tip: Detour worth it if you want quiet. Trailhead for the 39mi Resurrection Pass Trail and Sixmile Creek whitewater.

attractions.name: Kenai Fjords National Park Visitor Center
attractions.season: Open daily late May-early Sept; closed in winter
attractions.blurb: Small NPS center by the Seward small-boat harbor with the park film, ranger talks and backcountry/cabin permits.
attractions.tip: Stop here for bear-canister loans and Aialik/Holgate cabin permits before any boat drop - it is the only place to get them.
